greengrass.loggerdefinition.logger.Compo.Description: The source of the log event. Valid values are `GreengrassSystem` or `Lambda`. When `GreengrassSystem` is used, events from Greengrass system components are logged. When `Lambda` is used, events from user-defined Lambda functions are logged.

greengrass.loggerdefinition.logger.Id.Description: A descriptive or arbitrary ID for the logger. This value must be unique within the logger definition version. Maximum length is 128 characters with pattern `[a-zA-Z0-9:_-]+`.

greengrass.loggerdefinition.logger.Level.Description: The log-level threshold. Log events below this threshold are filtered out and aren't stored. Valid values are `DEBUG`, `INFO` (recommended), `WARN`, `ERROR`, or `FATAL`.

greengrass.loggerdefinition.logger.Space.Description: The amount of file space (in KB) to use when writing logs to the local file system. This property does not apply for CloudWatch Logs.

greengrass.loggerdefinition.logger.Type.Description: The storage mechanism for log events. Valid values are `FileSystem` or `AWSCloudWatch`. When `AWSCloudWatch` is used, log events are sent to CloudWatch Logs. When `FileSystem` is used, log events are stored on the local file system.
